L1.area: Random value generation and cryptographic utility functions

L2.description: A random digit generation routine uses a flawed mapping from random bytes to decimal digits, producing a biased distribution. The conversion arithmetic does not evenly partition the byte value space across the ten possible digit outputs, causing some digits to appear more frequently than others over a large number of invocations.

L3.area: Random value generation and cryptographic utility functions
L3.description: A random digit generation routine uses a flawed mapping from random bytes to decimal digits, producing a biased distribution. The conversion arithmetic does not evenly partition the byte value space across the ten possible digit outputs, causing some digits to appear more frequently than others over a large number of invocations.

ground_truth.reason: The randomDigits() function divides each random byte by 25.6 and floors the result to produce a digit 0-9, but since 256 is not evenly divisible by 10 this creates a biased distribution where some digits are more likely than others, undermining the cryptographic strength of generated values.
